element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to predict falling patterns and manage four independent stacks
Player learns matching mechanic: identical monsters eliminate each other on contact
Player controls Mario below field to swap adjacent stacks, core puzzle solving tool
Bottom halves behave like monsters; player learns to position them strategically
Top halves hatch Yoshi when contacting bottom halves, creating bonus point opportunities
Stacking monsters between top and bottom eggshell halves eliminates trapped monsters and spawns larger Yoshi
Initial blocks increase with each level; player must clear entire field rather than survive indefinitely

typical_relief_triggers: Large multi-block elimination reduces stack height
typical_tension_triggers: Stacks grow toward black line threshold
Falling monsters don't match positioned blocks
Top eggshell half falls without bottom half below (no points, block removed)
Game over imminent as stack approaches top
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave design: alternating challenging and easier stages allow player to savor accomplishment before difficulty increases again

difficulty_curve.early_game: A-Type: indefinite survival with basic monsters and learning stack swapping; B-Type: small initial field to clear
difficulty_curve.mid_game: B-Type: field complexity increases with each level; more monster types falling simultaneously; eggshell mechanics become critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: B-Type: field densely populated; player must execute precise swaps and multi-block elimination chains to clear completely
notable_spikes: First B-Type level transition from infinite A-Type survival mode
Mid-progression B-Type levels with exponentially growing initial block counts
Late B-Type levels requiring sustained focus and rapid decision-making

notable_level_patterns.description: Indefinite play until game over; no predefined end state; tests reflexes and sustained attention; single-player scoring focus
Series of levels with increasing initial block counts; clear entire field to advance; designed as discrete puzzle challenges
Two players (Mario vs Luigi) control separate fields; first to win three matches wins overall; parallel gameplay adds tension
